context.summary: A wireless alarm to warn you when someone opens a door √¢¬Ä¬ì or leaves it open.
how_it_works: The transmitter program uses the micro:bit√¢¬Ä¬ôs√Ç compass√Ç (magnetometer)√Ç input sensor and a√Ç loop√Ç to measure the strength of the magnetic field every two seconds.

It uses√Ç selection√Ç so when the magnetic field strength falls below a certain level (the threshold), it sends a√Ç radio√Ç signal √¢¬Ä¬òopen√¢¬Ä¬ô. If the magnetism reading goes above the threshold, it sends the signal √¢¬Ä¬òclosed'.

When the receiver micro:bit receives the signal √¢¬Ä¬òopen√¢¬Ä¬ô, a cross appears on its LED display and an audible alarm sounds. When it receives the signal √¢¬Ä¬òclosed√¢¬Ä¬ô, a tick appears on its LED display and no sound plays.
improve_it: Use multiple micro:bits to track the status of different doors by sending different radio messages, e.g. √¢¬Ä¬òback door open√¢¬Ä¬ô.
Use a variable to measure how long doors are left open √¢¬Ä¬ì could this help you save heating energy?

micropython.source:
from microbit import *
import radio
radio.config(group=17)
radio.on()

while True:
    if button_a.was_pressed():
        display.scroll(compass.get_field_strength())
    if compass.get_field_strength() < 100000:
        display.show(Image.DIAMOND_SMALL)
        radio.send('open')
    else:
        display.clear()
        radio.send('closed')
    sleep(2000)
